completion_promises.criterion_text: src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py exposes dispatch_by_capability(tool_name: str, payload_json: str, intent_pattern: str | None = None, timeout_seconds: int = 60) -> str decorated with @tool(parse_docstring=True).
completion_promises.evidence: Added @tool(parse_docstring=True) function with the exact signature in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py:193. TestAC001ToolExposure verifies the BaseTool wrapper exposes the four documented args via args_schema.

Docstring transcribed from docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/contracts/API-tools.md §3.1 with hyphens substituted for two en-dashes in the cost/latency line for ruff RUF002 compliance (semantic equivalence preserved). All headings, Args, and Returns sections match. Verified by TestAC002Docstring assertions on Resolution order/Use this tool when/error-string substrings.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Resolution: exact CapabilityToolSummary.tool_name match wins; else intent_pattern substring match on descriptor role/description with highest-confidence (first match on lexicographic agent_id order for stability); else ERROR: unresolved.
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented _resolve_agent_id helper that sorts descriptors by agent_id then iterates: exact CapabilityToolSummary.tool_name match first, then intent_pattern substring on descriptor.role/description. Returns None → ERROR: unresolved string. TestAC003Resolution covers exact match, lex tie-break, intent fallback, and unresolved cases.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Validates payload_json is a JSON object literal (starts with `{`, parses to dict). Non-object / non-JSON → ERROR: invalid_payload — payload_json is not a JSON object literal.
completion_promises.evidence: lstrip-then-startswith('{') guard + json.loads + isinstance(dict) check returns the documented error string verbatim. TestAC004PayloadValidation parametrises 7 invalid payloads (array, string, number, null, garbage, empty, malformed) and asserts the canonical error string.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Validates timeout_seconds in [5, 600]. Out of range → ERROR: invalid_timeout — timeout_seconds must be 5..600, got <n>.
completion_promises.evidence: Range guard returns the documented error string with the offending value interpolated. TestAC005TimeoutValidation parametrises out-of-range (0, 1, 4, 601, 10_000, -1) and in-range (5, 60, 600) values.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Constructs a real nats_core.events.CommandPayload with command=tool_name, args=json.loads(payload_json), correlation_id=new_correlation_id() (uses TASK-J002-005 helper).
completion_promises.evidence: Imports new_correlation_id from jarvis.tools._correlation and constructs CommandPayload(command=tool_name, args=parsed_args, correlation_id=correlation_id). TestAC006CommandPayloadConstruction wires a stub hook that captures the CommandPayload and asserts UUID4 shape + args round-trip.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Constructs a real MessageEnvelope(source_id='jarvis', event_type=EventType.COMMAND, correlation_id=..., payload=command.model_dump(mode='json')).
completion_promises.evidence: MessageEnvelope built with the exact kwargs; payload_bytes derived from envelope.model_dump_json(). TestAC007LogLineShape exercises the full path and asserts the rendered log line includes the resolved topic/payload_bytes derived from the envelope.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Emits exactly one logger.info call per invocation with message starting with LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH and containing tool_name=<x> agent_id=<y> correlation_id=<z> topic=agents.command.<y> payload_bytes=<n>.
completion_promises.evidence: Single logger.info call uses LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH constant as %s leading literal with all five required fields as positional args. TestAC007LogLineShape asserts exactly-one anchored log line per invocation and verifies all required fields.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Honours _stub_response_hook: unset → canned ResultPayload JSON with success=True, result={'stub':True,'tool_name':<x>}, correlation_id=<same>; timeout → TIMEOUT: agent_id=<y> tool_name=<x> timeout_seconds=<n>; specialist_error → ERROR: specialist_error — agent_id=<y> detail=<reason>.
completion_promises.evidence: Match statement handles all three StubResponse variants plus the unset-hook canned-success path. TestAC008StubResponseHook covers unset/timeout/specialist_error/success cases and asserts each documented return shape.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No retry inside the tool (DDR-009 §6); error string returned verbatim.
completion_promises.evidence: No retry/loop logic — every error path returns directly. The function emits a single log line per call (verified by TestAC007).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Concurrent dispatches produce distinct correlation IDs; two parallel invocations yield two distinct JARVIS_DISPATCH_STUB log lines, each carrying its own correlation_id.
completion_promises.evidence: Each call generates its own correlation_id via new_correlation_id() (CSPRNG, no shared state). TestAC009Concurrency spawns 5 threads, asserts 5 distinct correlation_ids in both log lines and ResultPayload returns.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Seam test: dispatches succeed end-to-end through assemble_tool_list-wired supervisor fixture; log capture verifies exactly-one log line per call.
completion_promises.evidence: End-to-end test via dispatch.dispatch_by_capability.invoke({...}) goes through the @tool BaseTool wrapper and asserts exactly-one anchored log line. assemble_tool_list itself lands in TASK-J002-015; the seam is closed by the bound_registry fixture which assigns to dispatch._capability_registry, the same hook assemble_tool_list will use.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Never raises; Pydantic ValidationError on MessageEnvelope construction is caught and returned as ERROR: validation — <detail>.
completion_promises.evidence: try/except around CommandPayload+MessageEnvelope construction catches ValidationError → 'ERROR: validation — <msg>'. Hook invocation wrapped in try/except → 'ERROR: specialist_error — agent_id=<y> detail=<exc>'. TestAC010NeverRaises covers the hook-raises and bad-args paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check on src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py reports 2 errors that are not from this task: UP017 in queue_build's _now_utc() helper (added by another task's autobuild pass) and UP040 on the pre-existing StubResponse TypeAlias (was in dispatch.py before this task). Net new lint errors introduced by this task: 0 (the 2 RUF002 en-dash issues introduced by the byte-for-byte docstring transcription were resolved by substituting hyphens). Baseline before the task had 5 errors; current count is 2.
